items.summary: PosTagger interface has one implementation (SimpleTagger) that is always referenced by its concrete type, making the interface earn no indirection benefit

evidence: All call sites use SimpleTagger.create() or new SimpleTagger() and assign to SimpleTagger variables — no variable is ever declared as PosTagger.
CLAUDE.md lists UDPipeTagger as a component but it does not exist in the source tree.
SimpleTagger.create() returns SimpleTagger (not PosTagger), leaking the concrete type at the factory boundary.
detail.suggestion: Either introduce the second tagger implementation the interface anticipates, or collapse SimpleTagger by removing the interface until a second implementation exists. If kept as an extension point, at minimum the factory methods should return PosTagger.

plan_cluster.name: abstraction-cleanup
plan_cluster.description: Remove hollow abstractions: dead Token match methods (7de930fa) and hollow PosTagger interface (5f270cc8).

action_steps.title: Delete matchesTag(), matchesLemma(), matchesWord(), matchesPosGroup() from Token.java — these methods duplicate AbstractPatternPredicate and are tested but not called in production [refs: 7de930fa, 3ce9ba20]
action_steps.detail: Token.java: matchesTag(), matchesLemma(), matchesWord(), matchesPosGroup() methods duplicate AbstractPatternPredicate logic. Only used in tests (TokenPatternMatchTest.java), never in production code path. Delete all four methods.

action_steps.title: Remove PosTagger interface; update Main.java and BlackLabConllUIndexer to use SimpleTagger directly — the interface adds indirection with no polymorphism benefit [refs: 5f270cc8, 66a1ad26]
action_steps.detail: PosTagger interface: only one implementation (SimpleTagger). Main.java and BlackLabConllUIndexer reference SimpleTagger directly already. Remove interface, keep SimpleTagger.

action_steps.title: Remove NotPredicate.negate() override if it duplicates the AbstractPatternPredicate default [refs: 66a1ad26]
action_steps.detail: NotPredicate.java: check if negate() override adds behavior beyond AbstractPatternPredicate default. If identical, remove the override.

action_steps.title: Evaluate RelationType enum in QueryExecutor: move to a standalone type or delete if unused [refs: ca604134, 81698ff1]
action_steps.detail: QueryExecutor.java: RelationType enum leaks CQL patterns into the query interface. Remove enum entirely or move to a standalone domain type if needed by GrammarConfigLoader.

items.summary: RelationType enum (grammatical relations) is nested inside QueryExecutor infrastructure interface

evidence: RelationType enum with values ADJ_MODIFIER, ADJ_PREDICATE, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF is a static nested type inside QueryExecutor
It carries CQL patterns and represents grammatical/linguistic domain knowledge, not query infrastructure
The grammar package already holds domain-level CQL concepts (CQLParser, CQLPattern)
detail.suggestion: Extract RelationType to its own top-level class in the grammar package (e.g., grammar.GrammaticalRelation) where it co-locates with other linguistic pattern definitions.

items.summary: NotPredicate class duplicates TokenPredicate.negate() default method; neither is clearly canonical

evidence: TokenPredicate declares a default negate() returning a lambda: token -> !this.test(token).
NotPredicate is a separate class doing the same thing with an added toString() method.
CQLVerifier line 192 uses new NotPredicate(pred) rather than pred.negate(), so the default method is never exercised in production.
detail.suggestion: Remove the negate() default from TokenPredicate (it is never called) and keep NotPredicate, which provides the useful toString() for debugging. Or keep negate() and replace new NotPredicate(pred) with pred.negate() throughout, then delete the class.

items.summary: QueryExecutor.RelationType embeds BCQL/CQL pattern strings inside the interface, leaking a concrete query language into the abstraction boundary

evidence: RelationType stores literal pattern strings like "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"] [lemma=\"%s\"]" — BlackLab CQL syntax baked into the interface shared by all implementors.
A hypothetical second QueryExecutor using a different pattern language cannot reuse RelationType without being forced to deal with CQL patterns it does not understand.
detail.suggestion: Move RelationType (with its CQL patterns) out of the QueryExecutor interface and into a BlackLab-specific or grammar-config layer. The interface should accept opaque pattern strings.

items.summary: WordSketchApiServer uses a full 5-method Builder inner class for a 4-field object constructed exactly once

evidence: Builder class with withExecutor/withIndexPath/withPort/withGrammarConfig/build methods; there is one call site in Main.java; no inheritance or optional-field benefit justifies the pattern
detail.suggestion: Replace with a plain constructor WordSketchApiServer(QueryExecutor, String, int, GrammarConfigLoader); delete the Builder inner class
